input.task: Build a simple workflow with a Manual Trigger node (the "When clicking 'Test workflow'" trigger that runs from the editor Test workflow button) connected to a Set node named "full execution test". The Set node does not need any fields configured — just the node present and connected.
input.conversationContext: User wants a minimal workflow: Manual Trigger → Set node named "full execution test".

output.result: STOP. Direct builder calls require `bypassPlan: true` + an existing `workflowId` + a one-sentence `reason`. Use that combination for any edit to an existing workflow. For new workflows, multi-workflow builds, or data-table schema changes, call `plan` with a `build-workflow` task instead — the planner discovers credentials, data tables, and best practices, and schedules an orchestrator-run verification checkpoint.

input.summary: A minimal manual-trigger workflow connected to a Set node, used for testing execution from the n8n editor.
assumptions: Manual Trigger node is used as the trigger (runs when the user clicks 'Test workflow' in the editor)
The Set node named 'full execution test' is left with no field assignments by default — the builder will wire it directly to the trigger output

item.name: Full Execution Test
item.purpose: A simple two-node workflow triggered manually from the n8n editor's 'Test workflow' button. When executed, it passes data through a Set node named 'full execution test', allowing the user to verify end-to-end execution from the editor.

A simple two-node workflow triggered manually from the n8n editor's 'Test workflow' button. When executed, it passes data through a Set node named 'full execution test', allowing the user to verify end-to-end execution from the editor.
